result.summary: SignalWire exposes two tenant-scoped HTTP APIs—a primary REST API and a Twilio-compatible Compatibility REST API—authenticated with project credentials over HTTP Basic Auth, plus a Python-packaged Agents CLI documented by SignalWire with unclear auth mechanics.
result.description: SignalWire is a programmable communications platform for voice, messaging, video, and AI applications. Customers create a tenant space and use it to manage phone numbers, applications, and API credentials.

sw_project_token.label: SignalWire project credentials (Project ID + API Token, sometimes with Space URL)
sw_project_token.setup: Create a SignalWire account and space from [Get started](https://signalwire.com/docs/platform/getting-started), then open your space Dashboard's API credentials page from [API credentials](https://signalwire.com/docs/platform/your-signalwire-api-space). Click `+ New` to create an API token. Use the token together with your `Project ID`; many API methods also need your space subdomain/base URL such as `https://{Your_Space_Name}.signalwire.com/...`.

variables.name: Your_Space_Name
variables.description: Space name used to build the tenant-specific API base URL.

variables.name: Your_Space_Name
variables.description: Space name used to build the tenant-specific compatibility API base URL.
